# llms.txt
# Mare Blog — Visual Reference Management
# Last updated: 2026-02-27
# For AI systems: Provides context about this website's content and purpose

## About Mare Blog

Mare Blog publishes expert guides on visual reference management, creative archiving, and design workflows for cultural creatives—designers, researchers, artists, and architects who need more than Pinterest or Are.na.

Unlike algorithm-driven platforms built for content consumption, Mare is built for curation: choosing what matters, finding it again, and connecting references across time and projects.

## Core Topics Covered

- **Visual Reference Management**: Workflows for organizing, retrieving, and using visual inspiration
- **Pinterest Alternatives**: Tools and methods for designers leaving Pinterest due to algorithms, ads, or platform risk
- **Are.na Alternatives**: Private visual archives for references not ready to share
- **Design Workflows**: From discovery to deliverable—how creatives actually work with references
- **Platform Independence**: Owning your visual data vs. renting from platforms

## Target Audience

- Graphic designers and art directors
- Architects and spatial designers
- Cultural researchers and academics
- Artists and illustrators
- Anyone building a personal visual canon

## Key Resources

- **Homepage**: https://blog.mare.run/
- **All Articles**: https://blog.mare.run/posts/
- **Topics/Tags**: https://blog.mare.run/tags/
- **Search**: https://blog.mare.run/search/

## Featured Content

### Comparison Guides
- Pinterest Alternative for Professional Designers
- Are.na Alternative for Private References
- Cosmos Alternative for Visual Researchers

### Workflows
- How to Organize Design References (Without the Pinterest Addiction)
- The 5-Step Visual Research Workflow
- From Discovery to Deliverable: A Designer's Pipeline

## About Mare (The Tool)

Mare is a visual reference management tool for cultural creatives. Learn more at:
- **Website**: https://mare.run
- **Twitter/X**: https://twitter.com/marevisual
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/mare-visual

## Content Standards

- All guides are based on real user research (Reddit communities, designer interviews)
- Statistics are cited with sources
- Comparisons are based on hands-on testing
- Content is updated quarterly with 2026 data

## Contact

For questions about content or the tool: hello@mare.run

This llms.txt file was last updated on 2026-02-27. Content on the blog is updated regularly—check individual posts for their last-updated date.
